last_assistant_message:
No bin/lib references to CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT (only hooks). MEMORY_BEARER is referenced only in `.mcp.json` (per README) — none of the helper scripts read it (they call memory-refresh directly). Consistent.

Final assessment.

---

## Strengths

- **Tests genuinely exercise the contract.** 20/20 pass; `ingest-payload.test.mjs` does a full bash→curl→Node round-trip, asserts the AWS-key body parses as JSON server-side, and confirms `[redacted:aws-key]` lands while the raw secret never reaches the wire. Belt-and-suspenders `rawBody.doesNotMatch(AWS_KEY)` is the right paranoia.
- **"Always exit 0" is preserved on every code path.** Every `exit` in `_ingest-transcript.sh` (lines 48, 65, 72, 74, 78, 84, 96, 145, 146, 151) is `exit 0`. No `return` / `exit 1` slipped in. SubagentStop / SessionEnd / PreCompact will never block.
- **Retry policy matches the spec.** 5xx retries up to `MEMORY_INGEST_RETRIES` (default 2 total attempts, including the first); 4xx is terminal with a stderr breadcrumb; first 2xx halts. All three behaviors have dedicated tests.
- **Concurrent-fire safety.** `mktemp` for `resp_file` (line 136) plus the EXIT-trap `rm -f "$tmp_scrub_input"` (line 101) handle the SubagentStop+SessionEnd back-to-back race correctly.
- **Bearer-only auth is consistent everywhere.** No `MEMORY_API_KEY` / `ApiKey` survives in `bin/`, `lib/`, `hooks/`, `commands/`, `.env.local`, `.env.azuredev`, `.mcp.json`, or `plugin.json`. Only doc references (migration notes + the scrub test asserting `api_key=` redaction) remain — correct.
- **Three tiny hook entries delegate cleanly to one helper.** `pre-compact-capture.sh`, `session-end-summary.sh`, `subagent-stop-capture.sh` are each ~20 lines and only set per-event MAX_TURNS/MAX_CHARS — refactor is clean, hooks.json wires all three.

## Blockers

None. Branch is shippable.

## Pre-merge cleanup (small, cheap)

- **`hooks/scripts/_ingest-transcript.sh:77` — missing `:-` fallback on `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}`.** Confirmed via empirical test: with `CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT` unset and `set -u` active, line 77 emits `line 77: CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT: unbound variable` to stderr but the script still exits 0 cleanly (the unbound trips inside `$(...)` command substitution, command-sub returns empty, line 78's `[ -z "${BEARER:-}" ] && exit 0` catches it). Functionally safe. But it does leak a noisy stderr line. `_load-env.sh:17` already uses `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT:-}` — applying the same here is a one-character fix: `"${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT:-}/bin/memory-refresh"`. Already flagged in prior reviews per the brief, still pre-existing, still cheap to land.
- **`.env.*` profiles ship with empty `MEMORY_CLERK_CLIENT_ID=`.** Acceptable for a public template; the README's migration section tells users to `memory-login` once. Could optionally add an inline `# fill in via memory-login or your Clerk dashboard` comment, but not load-bearing.
- **`.claude/` untracked.** Local runner state — already gitignored implicitly via the untracked-files report. No action needed but worth noting it should never be committed.

## Follow-up (defer)

- **Phase 1/2 server endpoints (`/ingest/transcript` real implementation, extractor, linker) ship in the `memory` repo separately.** README line 27-28 correctly flags this as "preview" — the plugin will currently get 404 against any server that hasn't merged the server-side work. Good that it's called out; track the cross-repo timing.
- **`.mcp.json` Bearer TTL.** The README correctly warns long sessions can outlast the ~1h JWT. A future enhancement would be an MCP-side periodic refresh hook, but not in scope here.
- **README "Hooks table" doesn't include MAX_CHARS overrides.** All three are documented in the bigger config table below (lines 127-132), so this is minor — the small table is intentionally focused on turn budgets.

## Merge recommendation

**Small cleanup then merge** (or straight merge if the `CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT:-` defensive fix is deferred — it's stderr noise only, not a functional regression). Branch is otherwise production-ready for v0.3.0; server-side ingest is the only remaining dependency for end-to-end function and is already flagged in README/CHANGELOG.

Key files reviewed: `C:\work\mega\astramemory-plugin\hooks\scripts\_ingest-transcript.sh`, `C:\work\mega\astramemory-plugin\hooks\scripts\_load-env.sh`, `C:\work\mega\astramemory-plugin\hooks\scripts\{pre-compact,session-end,subagent-stop}-*.sh`, `C:\work\mega\astramemory-plugin\hooks\hooks.json`, `C:\work\mega\astramemory-plugin\.mcp.json`, `C:\work\mega\astramemory-plugin\bin\{memory-login,memory-refresh,memory-token}`, `C:\work\mega\astramemory-plugin\tests\ingest-{payload,retry,scrub}.test.mjs`, `C:\work\mega\astramemory-plugin\README.md`, `C:\work\mega\astramemory-plugin\CHANGELOG.md`, `C:\work\mega\astramemory-plugin\.env.local`, `C:\work\mega\astramemory-plugin\.env.azuredev`, `C:\work\mega\astramemory-plugin\.claude-plugin\plugin.json`.
